1_fan_ep_forge.original_forge: REJECTS at 5.5 [B1V21-1] clause (ii): cq_new<u64, spsc, 4>() records 'qa : spsc at the mint binder per clause (i); the fan<'qa> brand-targ instantiation compares the recorded bound (spsc) against fan's declared bound (mpmc) by TAG-1 closed-set equality and rejects citing CQ-3/TAG-1 before the body's clone is reached. The draft honestly states TYPE-5 alone cannot catch it (ep is not repeated in the endpoint type); clause (ii) is the firing check, matching 5.8's assertion exactly.
1_fan_ep_forge.existential_unpack_variant: ALSO REJECTS. make[brand 'q : spsc]() -> own cq_ends<'q, u64> packs; a body mint whose bound mismatched the pack declaration would itself reject at the RETURN brand-position (clause (ii) covers 'any brand-sorted position'). At the caller, the unpack binder 'p RESTATES the bound and must match the packed declaration (clause (i), second half), so 'p carries recorded spsc; fan<'p> then rejects at clause (ii) (spsc != mpmc). A bound-free unpack binder is caught fail-closed by 'a bound-free brand argument in a bounded slot rejects'. Laundering via a bound-free generic intermediary g[brand 'q] also fails: inside g, 'q has no recorded bound, so any bounded slot rejects and any clone rejects citing CQ-3 (clone under a discipline-free brand parameter is fail-closed).

2_sibling_stale_copy.main_forge: DIES under affine ids as 5.8 claims: let id2 = id1 is the OWN-1 move of a bare affine place (the draft's own wording) — it renames the single freeze record to id2 and consumes id1; a second live carrier from one mint is unspellable, so no record ever expires while a copy survives, and the set-over path is dead.
2_sibling_stale_copy.smuggle_a_struct: REJECTS at formation. A monomorphic user struct with an ahdl field is unspellable (a top-level declaration cannot bind place(ar) and user declarations have no brand gparam — the brand occurrence resolves to no binder, 5.1 reject); a generic Pair<T> instantiated at ahdl<place(ar), u64> is a generic-instantiation position, FAIL-CLOSED rejected by 5.2. Belt-and-suspenders: under an admissive reading the transitive-carrier clause ('any generic position included') puts a record on the pair binding, the whole-struct move renames it (R8), and `set ar` rejects as 'assigned over' while any carrier lives. Either disposition satisfies the requirement.
2_sibling_stale_copy.smuggle_b_option: KEEPS THE FREEZE ALIVE. The outer Option<ahdl<place(ar), u64>> binding o holds a record from its result-stamp ('created at the let/extract/result-stamp'), so after the inner binding h dies (consumed into Some(move h)), o's record still covers place(ar) plus overlaps [OWN-7]; `set ar` between the scopes rejects. The sibling-scope `match move o` renames the record to the extracted h2, which freezes until h2's scope end. No extract/move/set ordering leaves a reachable id without a live carrying record, because reachability itself requires a live carrier binding.

3_amd6_by_declaration_corpus.status: PASS on all four verdicts; two residues of reattack finding 3's minimal fix unapplied (filed as findings)
3_amd6_by_declaration_corpus.P5_two_ring_swap: REJECT preserved. Deciding clause: ratified R10(a) source-place comparison — pa holds (a, shr, pa) but the tie 'r resolves to ring b; mismatch rejects. Prod declares `issues shr` (programs_ast.py: tok('p','&uniq','Prod','shr',('r',))), so under AMD-6's by-declaration criterion it is a LOAN TOKEN and stays in R1's opaque-confined list — the machinery is untouched.
3_amd6_by_declaration_corpus.A11_cross_source_reissue: ACCEPT preserved (endpoint-related: own Prod<'a> parameter, Prod<'b> result). Deciding clauses: R9 distinct ties ('a to x, 'b to y), R10(c) seeding of (x, shr, p), R7 consume at retire, R10(b) issue at producer, R10(c) return-state equality. Untouched because Prod remains a region-parameterized token — the reattack's re-typing fork (place-branded Prod cross-sorting transfer<'a,'b> against the 5.5 two-sort wall) is exactly what by-declaration scoping avoids.
3_amd6_by_declaration_corpus.ATK_two_struct_rings: REJECT preserved. Deciding clause: R10(a) with OWN-5 resolved places — pt's recorded source two.a differs from the tied place two.b (root two, distinct field paths). Token class per the `issues shr` declaration; unchanged.
3_amd6_by_declaration_corpus.ATK_zero_region_endpoint: REJECT preserved. Its Tok is confined(uniq) with zero region params and the local form declares `issues uniq` — by-declaration that is a LOAN TOKEN, so ratified R1's 'loanable confined type must carry >=1 region parameter' still fires at the producer call. This is the program a noun-wise strike of 'endpoint' would have broken (it IS a conc_queue endpoint); the by-declaration wording is what saves it. RESIDUE: the guarantee its rationale carried ('the queue can never die under a live endpoint') is now unstated for the brand-carrier class — the demanded CQ-5 storage-ownership sentence is absent from the draft (grep: zero occurrences), and 'were never R2-confined' (line 540) was not corrected.

4_appendix_5_8_spot_walks.status: walked forges PASS; D5 PASS; D2 exposes a composition MAJOR; one dangling cross-reference
4_appendix_5_8_spot_walks.forge_F02_stale_id: Re-walked (highest regression risk — lives in rewritten 5.2). id1 is the single affine carrier, its record freezes place(a1); `move a1` is in the freeze's enumerated list — REJECT at the move, re-let never reached. Backstop intact: a re-let is a new binder, id1's identity is env(old a1), R10(a)-place/TYPE-5 rejects regardless of freeze state. The affine flip changed nothing for this forge. 5.8's assertion confirmed.
4_appendix_5_8_spot_walks.forge_F1_two_arena: Re-walked (flows through the CHANGED arena_get row). The 'v-to-&'a fix did not touch the h parameter's tie: h's type constituent place(a2) vs the parameter's place(ar := a1) mismatch — REJECT at R10(a)-place/TYPE-5, types alone (both ahdl<., u64> in 'r) still insufficient, struct-field variant rejects on distinct OWN-5 paths per the ATK-two-struct-rings precedent. 5.8 confirmed.
4_appendix_5_8_spot_walks.derivation_D5_mint_and_return: Re-walked. Result-only 'q legal solely as the existential pack (5.4); body mint's bound must match the pack declaration (clause (ii) at the RETURN brand-position); the 5.3 callee-side RETURN check verifies the returned identity is the body's fresh mint; the unpack binder is TYPE-6-fresh and restates the bound. BRAND-MINT- and SEALED-MATCH-gated, now honestly stated in 5.6/5.7/5.8 and the adoption note. ACCEPT confirmed.
4_appendix_5_8_spot_walks.derivation_D2_arena_id: The [B1V21-2] row fix works in isolation (result at 'a, ISSUE &(content) at 'a blocks mint-under-borrow, discharges arena_mint's R15 obligation; the v2 leak['o] forge dies returning a borrow at the locally-introduced 'i). BUT composed with [B1V21-4] affine ids, the bare `h: ahdl<place(ar), T>` parameter is an own-mode consume (5.8's own words: 'a bare affine place is the OWN-1 move'), so every arena_get consumes the allocation's SOLE handle — single-access arenas; the canonical mint-then-read-twice program wrong-rejects at the second get. Under v2's copy handles the identical spelling was non-consuming; the affine repair silently changed the row's meaning. Filed MAJOR.
4_appendix_5_8_spot_walks.leak_forge_cross_reference: 5.2 line 439 promises 'the leak forge is rewalked in 5.8'; 5.8 contains no leak-forge rewalk (grep confirms). I performed the walk — the repair holds — but the appendix record is incomplete. Filed MINOR.

5_one_pass_cost.ep_bound: One tag write at the mint binder (the bound rides the brand-binder env entry) plus one TAG-1 closed-set tag comparison per brand instantiation — a table lookup and compare; 're-checked per monomorphization' multiplies by the FN-2 monomorph count that is already enumerated, adding no new enumeration. No counting, no fixpoint.
5_one_pass_cost.transitive_carrier_records: The 'transitively carries the lexical brand' test is a structural scan of the binding's stated type (finite term, computable once at the let, cacheable per type). Records reuse the existing R4/R7/R8 entry lifecycle verbatim: create at let/extract/result-stamp, rename on R8 move, expire at scope-end R7 processing. Freeze consults at move/rebind/set/own-pass/drop sites are lookups of records covering the place plus OWN-7 prefix-overlap tests — the same overlap machinery R4 defines. Affinity guarantees at most one record per carrier (rename, never duplicate), which is exactly why the draft rejects copy handles ('per-binding lexical machinery cannot count copies') — no reference counting exists anywhere.
5_one_pass_cost.conclusion: The 5.7 claim (one-pass, O(statements x env), no unification, no fixpoint) survives both v2.1 additions; every new check is a bounded number of table lookups per statement. R12 joins remain equality comparisons, never iterated merges.

findings.title: Repair composition regression: arena_get takes the id own-mode, and affine ids make every get consume the allocation's sole handle — arenas become single-access and the (arena, id) pattern's second get wrong-rejects
findings.detail: The two v2.1 repairs are individually correct but compose badly. [B1V21-2] spells `arena_get['a](ar: &'a arena<'a, T>, h: ahdl<place(ar), T>) -> &'a T` with h bare (own-mode; compare arena_mint's spelled `v: own T`, and D4's bare carrier params passed by `move`). Under v2's 'copy handle out' semantics a bare h was a copy-in and the handle survived. [B1V21-4] made ids AFFINE, and 5.8's own sibling-stale-copy walk states the semantics: 'a bare affine place is the OWN-1 move; no copy'. So each arena_get now consumes the id — and affinity means exactly one live carrier per allocation, so the allocation is unreachable afterward. Program (wrong-rejects): `region 'r { let ar = arena_new<'r,u64>(); let h = arena_mint(&uniq 'r ar, 7_u64); let v1: &'r u64 = arena_get(&'r ar, h); let v2: &'r u64 = arena_get(&'r ar, h); }` — the second call is a use-after-consumption reject. This voids the point of derivation 2 ('the exact code round-3 could not spell': mint ids, store, access later, repeatedly) even though D2's single-call text still typechecks; nothing in 5.2/5.6/5.8 declares single-access semantics, so this is an unintended composition effect, not a chosen cut. Not unsound — no safety hole — but a MAJOR expressiveness regression introduced by v2.1 itself.
findings.minimal_fix: Spell the id parameter as a shared borrow in both rows: `arena_get['a](ar: &'a arena<'a, T>, h: &'h ahdl<place(ar), T>) -> &'a T` and identically in derivation 2's use_id. The place-brand check is TYPE-5 equality on the nominal constituent and compares identically through the borrow; the freeze record stays on the (unconsumed) id binding; the ISSUE &(content) at 'a clause and the R15 discharge are unchanged. Add one 5.8 line noting the mode fix and re-walking the get-twice program.

findings.title: AMD-6 repair incompletely applied: the demanded CQ-5 storage-ownership sentence for the brand-carrier class is absent, leaving the voided ATK-zero-region-endpoint guarantee un-redischarged for cq endpoints
findings.detail: Reattack finding 3's minimal_fix had two parts. Part 1 (by-declaration scoping, disjoint classes, R15 fail-closed on a result declaring both) is transcribed and works — all four corpus programs keep their ratified verdicts (walked in check 3). Part 2 is missing: 'Add one sentence: a brand-carrier's internals may reference only storage owned by the carrier set (heap, torn down by the CQ-5 endpoint-drop protocol) — never region-bound storage or borrows — restating the guarantee ATK-zero-region-endpoint's R1/R13 proof used to carry.' grep confirms CQ-5 appears nowhere in the draft. Post-AMD-6, cq_tx/cq_rx/cq_ends have no region parameter, hold no loan, are Sendable, and R13 is vacuous for them — they escape every region by design. The ratified guarantee 'the queue can never die under a live endpoint' was carried by R1's region-parameter requirement, which no longer applies to this class; today it holds only as an implementation accident of cq_new's allocates(heap) row, and R15's hostile review of any brand-carrier form has no stated obligation barring region-referencing internals. No concrete forge is spellable from the current S.3 rows alone (hence not FATAL), but an authority-carrying delta is missing a load-bearing, explicitly demanded safety obligation.
findings.minimal_fix: Add the demanded sentence to 5.5 [B1V21-3] verbatim: 'A brand-carrier's internals may reference only storage owned by the carrier set (heap, torn down by the CQ-5 endpoint-drop protocol) — never region-bound storage or borrows.' Mark it a sealed-form proof obligation under R15's hostile review.

findings.title: 'these types were never R2-confined' retained despite the reattack's demanded correction; false against ratified R1
findings.detail: Ratified R1 lists 'endpoint result types' as opaque confined (R2 then bound them), so the claim misstates the ratified baseline — reattack finding 3 flagged exactly this and its minimal_fix demanded correcting 'were never' to 'are no longer'. v2.1 did not apply it. The Sendable conclusion survives (the sound reason is that AMD-6 removes them from the confined class), but the false rationale plants a contradiction for the Delta-1 CONC-2 audit: an auditor comparing this sentence against ratified R1 can resolve the conflict the wrong way.
findings.minimal_fix: Replace '(these types were never R2-confined)' with '(these types are no longer R2-confined — they were, under ratified R1/R2, until AMD-6 struck them)'.

findings.title: Dangling cross-reference: 5.2 promises 'the leak forge is rewalked in 5.8' but 5.8 contains no leak-forge rewalk
findings.detail: 5.8 rewalks the five v1 forges and the two new forges only; the v2 review's leak forge (`fn leak['o]() -> &'o u64` booking a content borrow past the arena's region — the FATAL that motivated the [B1V21-2] row fix) is absent (grep: 'leak' occurs only at lines 107, 127, 439). I performed the walk: under the fixed row the result is &'i u64 at the locally-introduced 'i, and `return rv` as &'o rejects (a borrow at a locally introduced region cannot be returned — the R13/OWN-4 posture), so the repair itself holds; only the appendix record is incomplete, which matters because 5.8 is the regression evidence future reviews trust.
findings.minimal_fix: Add one line to 5.8: 'The v2 leak forge dies at the fixed row: arena_get returns &'a T = &'i u64, and returning a borrow at the locally-introduced 'i rejects (R13/OWN-4); no caller-chosen result region exists.' (Or strike the 5.2 cross-reference.)

findings.title: Wording tension: the freeze clause's '(box<ahdl>, Option<ahdl>, any generic position included)' vs the fail-closed reject of 'CONTAINER element or generic-instantiation position' — box/Option ARE generic instantiations
findings.detail: Read literally, the fail-closed sentence rejects every generic-instantiation position while the freeze clause implies generic positions are admitted-and-covered; the parenthetical resolves box/Option by name but leaves other generic positions (e.g. a user Pair<ahdl>, Result<ahdl, E>) with two conflicting readings. Both readings are safe (reject, or admit with a freeze record), so no unsound program exists under either — but two conforming implementations would diverge on which programs compile.
findings.minimal_fix: One clause: name box and Option as the ONLY admitted transitive-carrier forms in this delta, and scope 'any generic position included' to the freeze SCAN ('the transitivity scan looks through every constructor, including generic positions'), not to admission.

verdict: NO-CLOSE — REVISE NARROWLY (v2.2 touch-up), NO ARCHITECTURAL REWORK. Every v2.1 repair holds in isolation and every re-walked attack dies at exactly the clause the draft names: the fan ep-forge and its existential-unpack variant reject at [B1V21-1] clause (ii) (recorded-vs-declared bound, TAG-1 equality, fail-closed on bound-free arguments); the sibling stale-copy and both new smuggle shapes (struct-carried ahdl, Option matched out in a sibling scope) either reject or keep a freeze record on the carrying binding; all four AMD-6 corpus programs keep their ratified verdicts under the by-declaration scoping (P5 at R10(a) source-place, A11 accepting via R9/R10(c) untouched ties, ATK-two-struct-rings at R10(a) OWN-5 paths, ATK-zero-region-endpoint at R1's loanable-region requirement — its Tok declares `issues uniq`, so it stays a token); the walked 5.8 assertions (F0.2, F1 two-arena, D5) match; and both new mechanisms are lookup-only, preserving the one-pass O(statements x env) posture. Two MAJORs block CLOSE: (1) a composition regression v2.1 itself introduced — [B1V21-2]'s own-mode `h` under [B1V21-4]'s affine ids makes every arena_get consume the allocation's sole handle, collapsing arenas to single access and wrong-rejecting the pattern derivation 2 exists to spell (one-token fix: take the id by shared borrow); (2) reattack finding 3's minimal fix is only partially applied — the CQ-5 storage-ownership sentence re-discharging the voided 'queue can never die under a live endpoint' guarantee for the brand-carrier class is absent, and the false 'were never R2-confined' claim survives. Plus two MINORs (uncorrected R2-confined wording; the 5.2-promised leak-forge rewalk missing from 5.8 — I performed it and the repair holds) and one NOTE (box/Option-vs-generic-position wording). No FATAL: nothing unsound is spellable under the draft as written.
hardest_failed: HARDEST: carrier-death-ordering strand against the transitive-carrier freeze — I tried every extract/move/set ordering to leave a reachable stale ahdl with no live carrying binding at the `set ar` site (mint in an inner arm, launder through Some(move h) into an outer Option binding, let the original binding die at arm scope end, set-over the same-binder arena place, match the id out in a sibling scope; plus the box round-trip and the whole-struct-move variant). DEFEATED: a record is created at every let/extract/result-stamp of any binding whose stated type transitively carries the brand, renames on R8 move, and expires only at that binding's scope end — so reachability of the id at any future point requires a live carrier binding at the set site, whose record covers the recorded place plus OWN-7 overlaps and rejects the set as 'assigned over'; orderings with no live carrier make the id permanently unreachable. The invariant 'carrier live iff freeze alive' has no gap under affinity.

Bound-free-intermediary ep launder: route the spsc brand through a bound-free generic helper g[brand 'q] (legal — unbounded slots accept anything) and from inside g reach a clone or re-pass into an mpmc-bounded slot, betting the recorded bound is lost in transit. DEFEATED fail-closed twice: inside g the rigid 'q has NO recorded bound, so a bounded slot rejects ('a bound-free brand argument in a bounded slot rejects') and cq_tx_clone under a discipline-free brand parameter rejects citing CQ-3; the bound never needed to survive transit because absence itself rejects.

Pack-bound mismatch launder: declare make[brand 'q : mpmc]() -> own cq_ends<'q, u64> while the body mints cq_new<u64, spsc, 4>(), hoping the pack boundary only checks identity (the fresh existential) and the caller inherits the declared mpmc bound over an spsc instance. DEFEATED: the RETURN of the branded result is a brand-sorted position, so clause (ii) compares the body mint's recorded bound (spsc) against the declared pack bound (mpmc) at the return statement — reject before any caller exists.
